target: M1 verification batch — drain four single-line factual/wording corrections from the orphan queue per HNOTE-OPERATOR-DRAIN-M1-ORPHAN-QUEUE-001 (high). Each patch is a verbatim search-and-replace; no new arguments are introduced; existing claim structures are unchanged.
scope_summary: Four narrow text patches across data/sections.json (Part 5.0 OTS canary anchor) and data/wins.json (WIN-053 detail_evidence; WIN-062 detail_verdict_text). Each patch is single-sentence-or-shorter scope. No verdict changes, no new sections, no schema additions. Curmudgeon Step 8a is welcome to batch-review with sibling verification EXPs.

patches.target_locator: part5.html — the OTS-canary 'Companion concession' paragraph in Part 5.0 c1, where the anchor `<a href="#timestamp-error" onclick="showTab('timestamp');return false">` precedes the bold text 'Companion concession on the OTS canary.'
patches.rationale: ISS-1219 finding (a/b): the `;return false` cancels default href navigation so the reader lands at the top of the Timestamp Error tab rather than at the #timestamp-error anchor. Removing `;return false` preserves the tab switch (showTab fires synchronously) and lets the browser's default anchor jump complete. This matches the analogous already-corrected anchors in WIN-058, WIN-058b, and WIN-062 detail_evidence (verified via grep: those three WIN files have plain `onclick="showTab('timestamp')"` without `;return false` while sections.json still carries the older idiom). The ISS-1219 (b) ask — adding a stable subsection id `ots-2026-04-17-concession` inside the Timestamp Error tab — is intentionally deferred to a separate EXP because that requires a coordinated edit (id placement + href update) and is broader than verification class. The `;return false` removal is the higher-value half of the fix and is fully verification-class scope.
patches.regression_risk: Low. The href is unchanged; only the JS short-circuit is removed. If JS is disabled the link still navigates to the anchor (it did before too — `showTab` no-ops without JS — but `return false` would never have fired without JS either, so behavior with-JS-on changes from 'tab-switch only' to 'tab-switch + anchor jump'). Aligns with sibling-WIN behavior.

patches.target_locator: WIN-053 detail_evidence — the parenthetical describing the southern-pole concession mechanism. Sentence reads: 'That mechanism — multi-layer crustal magnetic conduction with depth-dependent susceptibility — is exactly the territory standard geomagnetism handles with degree-13 spherical harmonics anchored in geodynamo physics, and is precisely the territory the dome was constructed to leave behind.'
patches.current_text: That mechanism — multi-layer crustal magnetic conduction with depth-dependent susceptibility — is exactly the territory standard geomagnetism handles with degree-13 spherical harmonics anchored in geodynamo physics, and is precisely the territory the dome was constructed to leave behind.
patches.proposed_text: That mechanism — potential-field continuation of the deep-source geodynamo signal upward through mantle and crust, modulated by depth-dependent crustal susceptibility — is exactly the territory standard geomagnetism handles with degree-13 spherical harmonics anchored in geodynamo physics (IGRF / WMM-class models), and is precisely the territory the dome was constructed to leave behind.
patches.rationale: ISS-1305 finding: 'multi-layer crustal magnetic conduction' is technically imprecise. Standard geomagnetism handles deep-source-to-surface fields via potential-field continuation under Maxwell-magnetostatic boundary conditions (the field is curl-free outside source regions, so it satisfies Laplace's equation and admits spherical-harmonic expansion). 'Conduction' would imply a current-carrying medium, which is the wrong physical mechanism. The depth-dependent susceptibility note is preserved because it captures the crustal-modulation contribution (lithospheric magnetisation), but it's now correctly framed as a modulator on top of the continuation, not as the primary transport mechanism. The sentence stays the same length and keeps the rhetorical structure (mechanism — territory standard geomagnetism handles — territory dome was constructed to leave behind).
patches.regression_risk: Low. The argumentative thrust is unchanged: the sentence still asserts that the southern coefficient describes territory standard geomagnetism handles competently and the dome cannot reach. Wording precision improves; no claim added or removed.

patches.target_locator: WIN-062 detail_verdict_text — the post-hoc analysis paragraph. Current sentence: 'The value has been in the scientific record for over a century.'
patches.current_text: The value has been in the scientific record for over a century.
patches.proposed_text: The measurement is over a century old (Tesla 1899) and has been publicly accessible since the 1978 posthumous publication at the latest.
patches.rationale: ISS-1247 finding: 'in the scientific record for over a century' is accurate for the 1899 measurement but lets a pedantic defender push back on what counts as 'scientific record' — Tesla's contemporaneous notes were private until the 1978 Beograd publication. Tightening to two anchored dates (1899 measurement, 1978 posthumous publication) closes that pivot. The argumentative weight is unchanged; the carve-out test in Part 3.1b only requires that the value was publicly knowable at write-time, and the 1978 date is well before 2025-2026.
patches.regression_risk: Low. Numerical anchors are sourced from the same Tesla / Colorado Springs Notes context already cited in adjacent sentences (1899 Colorado Springs experiment date; Beograd 1978 posthumous publication). No new claim, narrower phrasing.

patches.target_locator: WIN-062 detail_verdict_text — the same paragraph as PATCH-302-C, sentence: '...labels it CONFIRMED, without pre-registering the expected value or identifying a novel measurement that could not have been known at write-time.' (the phrase 'without pre-registering the expected value' is the patch target)
patches.current_text: without pre-registering the expected value or identifying a novel measurement
patches.proposed_text: without pre-registering the expected value in any independent timestamping venue (see Timestamp Error tab for why the dome's OTS practice anchors status_history.json rather than the predictions themselves) or identifying a novel measurement
patches.rationale: ISS-1248 finding: a dome defender can rebut 'without pre-registering' by pointing to their OpenTimestamps anchors. Adding the 'in any independent timestamping venue' qualifier plus a Timestamp Error tab cross-reference closes that rebuttal pre-emptively — the dome's OTS hashes status_history.json (an observation file) not the predictions themselves, which is the OTS-wrong-side argument we make on the Timestamp Error tab. The cross-link uses the same `<a href="#timestamp-error" onclick="showTab('timestamp')">` idiom (no `;return false`) consistent with the now-canonical pattern in WIN-058/058b/062 (and after PATCH-302-A, sections.json Part 5.0 c1).
patches.regression_risk: Low. The argumentative claim is unchanged (no pre-registration in independent venue). The cross-link is consistent with existing in-WIN cross-links to the same tab. The Timestamp Error tab covers the OTS-wrong-side argument with the rigour needed; the cross-link does not introduce new content here.

obe_resolutions.finding: ISS-1259 reported `onclick="showTab(timestamp);return false"` on WIN-062 detail_evidence anchors cancelling the anchor jump. Verified via grep against current data/wins.json: WIN-062, WIN-058, and WIN-058b detail_evidence anchors to #timestamp-error are now `onclick="showTab('timestamp')"` without `;return false`. The fix the ISS asked for has already been applied in a prior commit; the parallel SEC-5.0 anchor (still has `;return false`) is being addressed by PATCH-302-A above.
obe_resolutions.evidence: grep -oE '<a [^>]*timestamp-error[^>]*>' data/wins.json yields zero hits with `;return false` in the matching anchors.

obe_resolutions.finding: ISS-1218 asked to soften the Part 5.0 c1 TLDR phrase 'the demoted rows are precisely the two post-window rows on a six-row page' to 'exactly the two retrospective/post-window rows; the four unchanged rows are all rows whose verdict windows are still open or only partially closed.' The first half of that softening has been applied in a prior commit — current text reads 'the demoted rows are exactly the two retrospective/post-window rows on a six-row page'. The second half (the explicit clarification about the four unchanged rows) was not added; on review, the four-rows clause introduces a substantive new claim about all six rows and is closer to deep-attack scope than verification (it requires the analyst to defend the characterisation of all four pending rows as 'still open or only partially closed', which is true but invites adjacent challenges). Recommend closing the verification-half as completed-elsewhere; the four-rows clarification can be raised as a fresh deep-attack ISS later if curmudgeon flags it on a future c-cycle.
obe_resolutions.evidence: Current sections.json text: 'the demoted rows are exactly the two retrospective/post-window rows on a six-row page' (matches ISS-1218's recommended softer wording for the first half).

obe_resolutions.finding: ISS-1304 asked to verify DOI 10.1029/2019JB017997 (Pappa et al. 2019, JGR). Verified via web search: the DOI resolves to Pappa, F., Ebbing, J., Ferraccioli, F., & van der Wal, W. (2019). 'Modeling Satellite Gravity Gradient Data to Derive Density, Temperature, and Viscosity Structure of the Antarctic Lithosphere.' Journal of Geophysical Research: Solid Earth, 124(11). The citation in WIN-067 detail_verdict_text and the parallel hyperlink at https://doi.org/10.1029/2019JB017997 are correct as published. No replacement to Ferraccioli et al. 2011 is needed — the existing citation lands on the correct paper and the correct authors.
obe_resolutions.evidence: Wiley Online Library / NORA (NERC Open Research Archive) confirm publication and DOI; Pappa et al. is one of two 2019 Pappa-led papers (the other is the Moho-depths paper, DOI 10.1029/2018GC008111, in G-cubed) — both are East Antarctic gravity-derived crustal/mantle work and either would be a valid citation; WIN-067 cites the Solid Earth paper which is the more directly relevant of the two.

holes_addressed: ISS-1219 (a) — JS-required href cancellation removed from SEC-5.0 OTS canary anchor (PATCH-302-A)
ISS-1305 — replaces 'magnetic conduction' (incorrect mechanism) with 'potential-field continuation' (correct standard-geomagnetism mechanism) in WIN-053 detail_evidence (PATCH-302-B)
ISS-1247 — tightens 'in the scientific record for over a century' to dated 1899/1978 anchors in WIN-062 detail_verdict_text (PATCH-302-C)
ISS-1248 — adds 'in any independent timestamping venue' qualifier + Timestamp Error tab cross-link to WIN-062 'pre-registering' phrase (PATCH-302-D)
ISS-1259 — verified OBE; WIN-062/058/058b detail_evidence anchors no longer carry `;return false` (no patch needed; close as resolved-already)
ISS-1218 — verified partial-OBE; first-half softening applied previously, second-half clarification deferred as deep-attack (close as partial-done)
ISS-1304 — DOI verified valid via web search (close as verified-valid)
new_evidence_added: Pappa et al. 2019 DOI verification via Wiley/NORA web sources for ISS-1304

anticipated_objections.objection: Curmudgeon: 'The 1899/1978 dating of Tesla's measurement in PATCH-302-C cites no inline source.'
anticipated_objections.response: Both anchors are already in the surrounding sentences of WIN-062 detail_verdict_text — the 1899 Colorado Springs experiment date and the Beograd 1978 posthumous publication are already cited two sentences earlier in the same paragraph (Tesla's Colorado Springs Notes, Beograd 1978). The patch reuses already-anchored numbers; no new sourcing is needed.

anticipated_objections.objection: Curmudgeon: 'Removing `;return false` in PATCH-302-A could double-fire navigation in some browsers.'
anticipated_objections.response: Empirically tested by the analogous WIN-058/058b/062 anchors that already lack `;return false` and have been live in production docs/index.html. No double-fire reports in any prior c-review of those WINs. The browser default after JS click handler returns is to follow the href; this is well-defined behavior across all modern browsers.

anticipated_objections.objection: Curmudgeon: 'PATCH-302-B trades one approximation ("conduction") for another ("potential-field continuation") — but susceptibility is a constitutive parameter for magnetised material, not for the harmonic continuation itself.'
anticipated_objections.response: The proposed wording explicitly separates the two: potential-field continuation is the upward-transport mechanism, and depth-dependent crustal susceptibility is a modulator on the continuation (the lithospheric magnetisation contribution). This matches the geomagnetism textbook framing (e.g., Blakely 1996; Backus, Parker & Constable 1996): main field continues from the core via potential theory; crustal field is layered on as magnetised-rock magnetisation.

anticipated_objections.objection: Curmudgeon: 'PATCH-302-D adds a cross-link that the Timestamp Error tab argument may not fully support — the OTS-wrong-side argument is about timestamping observations vs predictions, but the dome could still pre-register Tesla 1.574c by hashing it as a single-line text file.'
anticipated_objections.response: Yes, exactly — that's the point. The dome COULD pre-register but DIDN'T. The Timestamp Error tab argues that the dome's actual practice does not anchor predictions, only observation states. Saying 'no pre-registration in any independent timestamping venue' is precisely accurate against the dome's actual OTS usage; pointing the reader at the Timestamp Error tab gives them the receipts.

kernel_of_truth: All four ISSs and the three OBE resolutions reflect curmudgeon's accurate read of small precision gaps in our prose. None is a deep-attack; the curmudgeon-c5 generation has gotten good at flagging the kind of phrasing-pivot a sharp dome defender would exploit. Resolving them keeps the rhetorical surface tight without changing any verdict.
cross_references: ISS-1219 (a) addressed; (b) — adding stable subsection id `ots-2026-04-17-concession` to the Timestamp Error tab — deferred as a follow-up EXP
ISS-1259 — verified OBE; close-as-resolved-already proposal accompanies this EXP
ISS-1218 — verified partial-OBE; close-as-partial-done proposal accompanies this EXP. Four-rows clarification could be re-raised as deep-attack ISS later
ISS-1304 — verified valid; close-as-verified-valid proposal accompanies this EXP
scope_notes: All four patches and three OBE resolutions are NARROW (single-line / single-sentence) and target NOT_NEVER_PUSH files (data/sections.json, data/wins.json). NARROWNESS gate passes for each. RE-GREP gate passes (verbatim current_text was located in target file by direct grep at authoring time). Curmudgeon Step 8a is welcome to batch-review with sibling verification EXPs (≤20KB combined diff).
test_recommendations: Manual smoke test: build site, click the Part 5.0 c1 OTS-canary 'Companion concession' anchor, confirm reader lands at #timestamp-error subsection (not top of tab). Same for the Timestamp Error tab cross-link added in PATCH-302-D.
tests/run.sh — none of these patches touch numeric data, classifier, ransomware/http_data, or per-WIN counts. Standard test pass should be sufficient.
JSON validation: `node -e "JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('data/sections.json','utf8'));console.log('sections valid')"` and similarly for wins.json after applying patches.
curmudgeon_routing_note: review_class: 'verification' — batchable in Step 8a with up to 2 sibling verification EXPs (≤20KB combined diff). No new arguments or verdict changes; narrow factual/wording precision tightenings only. Source: HNOTE-OPERATOR-DRAIN-M1-ORPHAN-QUEUE-001 mandates verification-batch authoring.
